region_of_origin: Korea (Uijeongbu, Gyeonggi Province) — originated post-Korean War (1950–1953) near US Army bases
food_subgroup: Kimchi-gochujang broth stew with Spam, Frankfurt-style sausages, instant ramyeon noodles, firm tofu, and baked beans

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no dedicated concept for Budae Jjigae. Closest parent is a mixed protein stew with noodles (FOODON:03315595 — stew). No exact URI available; null is appropriate.
ontology.foodex2_description: Korean army base stew (budae jjigae) — Spam, frankfurters, kimchi, instant noodles, tofu, baked beans in gochujang-anchovy broth; FoodEx2 closest parent match used as no exact code exists for this composite dish.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for Budae Jjigae. A0BDF (mixed meat and vegetable stew) used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Budae Jjigae as a specific food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One individual serving of Budae Jjigae is approximately 500g, representing a per-person share of a communal hot pot typically served at the table in Korean restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — component-based estimation: Spam Classic (FDC ID 1635), Frankfurter pork and beef (FDC ID 172949), Kimchi (FDC ID 169999), Instant ramen noodles cooked (FDC ID 172452), Firm tofu (FDC ID 172476), Baked beans (FDC ID 173744); cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo database for composite stew values
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Budae Jjigae as a composite dish. Values derived from weighted component analysis using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for Spam, frankfurters, kimchi, instant ramen noodles, firm tofu, and baked beans, proportioned to a representative recipe. Cross-referenced with HPB Singapore database for Korean stew category benchmarks. Input record values (energy 112 kcal, protein 7.2g, fat 5.5g, carbohydrate 9.5g, sodium 720mg per 100g) are consistent with this component-based analysis and are retained. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat data not available at composite level; set to -1.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Mixed Stews and Noodle Dishes — analogous estimate based on instant ramen noodles (GI ~60, University of Sydney GI Database) moderated by high protein (Spam, frankfurters, tofu) and legume components (baked beans GI ~40); composite GI estimated at 50–55
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Budae Jjigae in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 52 (low range) based on: (1) instant ramen noodles as primary starch source (GI ~60, University of Sydney GI Database); (2) protein-fat dampening effect from Spam, frankfurters, and tofu reducing postprandial glucose response; (3) baked beans component (GI ~40) lowering composite GI; (4) kimchi fermentation acids further attenuating glycemic response. GL per 500g serving = (52 × 9.5g carbohydrate per 100g × 500g / 100) / 100 = 24.7g. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Extreme sodium load — 720mg per 100g; a 500g serving delivers approximately 3,600mg sodium, exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000mg by 80% in a single dish
hypertension.guidance: Budae Jjigae is contraindicated for patients with hypertension due to its extreme sodium content (720mg/100g) from simultaneous high-sodium sources including Spam (~1,370mg/100g), frankfurters (~850mg/100g), instant ramen seasoning (~1,200mg per pack), kimchi (~670mg/100g), doenjang, and gochujang; patients with hypertension should avoid this dish or consume only a very small portion (under 100g) with no added seasoning.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL 24.7 per 500g serving) from instant ramen noodles as primary starch, partially offset by high protein from Spam, frankfurters, and tofu, and legume fibre from baked beans
type2_diabetes.guidance: Budae Jjigae has a moderate glycemic load (GL ~24.7 per standard 500g serving) driven by instant ramen noodles (GI ~60), but the high protein and fat content from processed meats and tofu attenuates postprandial glucose response; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 200–250g, reduce or omit the noodle component, and monitor blood glucose, while noting that the very high sodium content poses an additional cardiovascular risk in this population.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combined cardiovascular risk from extreme sodium (720mg/100g), saturated fat (2.0g/100g), trans fat (0.1g/100g), cholesterol (25mg/100g), and ultra-processed meat nitrates from Spam and frankfurters
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Budae Jjigae presents a high cardiovascular risk profile due to its extreme sodium content (3,600mg per 500g serving), saturated fat, trans fat, and nitrate-containing ultra-processed meats (Spam and frankfurters), all of which are independently associated with increased cardiovascular disease risk; patients with established cardiovascular disease or high cardiovascular risk should avoid this dish, and if consumed for cultural reasons, should strictly limit portion size, omit Spam and frankfurters, and increase tofu and vegetable proportions.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Proinflammatory combination of ultra-processed meats with nitrates/nitrites, trans fat, and extreme sodium load
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Budae Jjigae is strongly cautioned for patients with rheumatoid arthritis due to its proinflammatory profile: ultra-processed meats (Spam, frankfurters) contain nitrates/nitrites that activate the NF-κB inflammatory pathway, trans fat (0.1g/100g) promotes systemic inflammation, and extreme sodium further exacerbates inflammatory burden; if consuming for cultural reasons, patients should minimise Spam and frankfurter portions and increase kimchi, tofu, and mushroom proportions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Budae Jjigae (부대찌개, literally 'army base stew') is one of Korea's most historically distinctive and culturally resonant dishes — a vivid example of culinary fusion born from wartime hardship. During and after the Korean War (1950–1953), food was scarce in Korea, but surplus US military rations — Spam, frankfurters, canned beans, and instant noodles — were available near US army bases in cities such as Uijeongbu and Pyeongtaek. Korean cooks combined these American processed foods with Korean kimchi, gochujang (red chilli paste), and doenjang (fermented soybean paste) broth, creating a spicy, bubbling, deeply savoury stew that was both filling and warming. What began as wartime improvisation became comfort food: by the 1960s–70s, budae jjigae restaurants proliferated near army bases, and today it is beloved nostalgic 'retro Korean food'. Served in a communal pot at the table, it bubbles with Spam, sausages, kimchi, tofu, instant ramyeon noodles, mushrooms, and baked beans, often topped with a melting American processed cheese slice. The flavour is an extraordinary collision of Korean fermented spice, American processed-meat saltiness, and cheese creaminess. Nutritionally, Budae Jjigae is one of the highest-sodium dishes in Korean cuisine: 720mg sodium per 100g, delivering approximately 3,600mg per 500g serving — exceeding the WHO daily sodium limit in a single meal. It is high in protein (7.2g/100g) from multiple meat and tofu sources, moderate in fat (5.5g/100g), and moderate in carbohydrate (9.5g/100g) primarily from instant noodles. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low) due to protein-fat dampening of the noodle starch. Clinically, this dish is high concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to extreme sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and ultra-processed meat nitrates. It is moderate concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to moderate glycemic load (GL ~24.7 per serving). It is strongly cautioned for rheumatoid arthritis patients due to its proinflammatory ultra-processed meat and sodium profile.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Budae Jjigae as a composite dish. Nutrient values are derived from component-based analysis using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for Spam, frankfurters, kimchi, instant ramen noodles, firm tofu, and baked beans, cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Korean stew benchmarks. Input record values are consistent with this analysis and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component data. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact approved-source match exists for this composite dish.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Budae Jjigae as a composite dish are not available in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore database at the composite level as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite stew preparation is not reported in available approved sources. All other nutrient fields are populated from component-based USDA FoodData Central analysis. Trans fat (0.1g/100g) is present from processed pork products (Spam, frankfurters). Sodium is critically high at 720mg/100g; a 500g serving delivers approximately 3,600mg sodium. GI value of 52 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for instant ramen noodles (GI ~60) and baked beans (GI ~40), moderated by high protein-fat content; not a direct measurement. GL per 500g serving calculated as (52 × 9.5 × 500/100) / 100 = 24.7. FoodEx2 code A0BDF used as closest parent match; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for Budae Jjigae. FoodOn URI is null as no dedicated FoodOn concept exists for this dish. SNOMED CT code is null as no specific food entity concept exists. The alias '부대찌개' is listed under Korean language; it also appears in the input as language 'en' which is corrected here to 'ko'. All aliases_by_language string values are confirmed present verbatim in the flat aliases array.
